thesis: Harmonic is a broadband infrastructure pure-play after divesting its video business. Q1 2026 broadband revenue surged 43% YoY with $582M backlog providing exceptional visibility. FY2026 guidance raised to $475-495M revenue and $0.57-0.67 EPS. The $200M buyback and pending video sale to MediaKind sharpen focus on the higher-growth broadband segment. Trading at ~24.5x forward P/E with a sticky software platform and fiber upgrade tailwinds.

drivers: Broadband revenue sustains 30%+ growth with backlog conversion
Video sale closes at premium valuation, bolstering balance sheet
Margins expand to 55%+ as memory costs normalize and software mix rises
Multiple re-rating to 30x P/E on pure-play broadband status

risks: Broadband growth decelerates to 10% as cable operator capex slows
Memory costs and new product ramps persistently compress margins
Competitive pressure from Calix, Adtran, and Nokia intensifies
Video sale delays or falls through, creating strategic uncertainty

base_case.assumptions: Broadband grows 20-25% YoY; video sale closes as planned in H2 2026; gross margins stabilize at 51-52%; company trades at 25x forward P/E. Base case implies 5-18% upside from current price with backlog-supported visibility.

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings — broadband momentum and video sale update
Video business sale to MediaKind expected close
Q3 2026 earnings — potential guidance raise if backlog converts

key_risks: Customer concentration among major cable operators
Competition from Calix, Adtran, and Nokia in broadband access
Margin pressure from memory costs and new product ramps
